title: The Ancient Kingdom’s Shadow
description: On pages 5–7, this part focuses on Nami, Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5, Dorry, Shanks, Unknown person #6 as Vegapunk’s satellites reveal the island’s technology and ancient past while CP0 approaches and Bonney pursues the truth about Kuma.
scene: Vegapunk’s satellites reveal the island’s technology and ancient past while CP0 approaches and Bonney pursues the truth about Kuma.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Let's look for some future treasure!:)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hmm, like a flower made entirely out of exquisite antique gems!:)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can't tell if your taste is closer to a little girl or an old hag...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Doesn't he look a little too young?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, my first guess would be he's his son...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yeah, but on second thought, maybe jinbe was somehow turned into a kid?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Nah, I'm going with option three. It's a coincidence!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I mean, the kid's got wings. Although, there's an ominous air about him...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: out with it, who the heck are you?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He may look like a kid, but he's just as big as the real jinbe!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I've got a bad feelin'... there's a fourth possibility!!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: This was all one giant trap!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We should've known the government was never gonna roll out the Red Carpet!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...they're using a clone of jinbe as a soldier?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I really don't want to fight him!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Should I pop out to help, Nami?

summary: This part advances the Egghead / Vegapunk Truth through 36 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
